input:
NET-METERING BILLING SYSTEM
NAME OF APPLICATION
Net-Metering Billing System (NMBS)
1.0 ABSTRACT
The Net-Metering Billing System is a comprehensive, enterprise-grade web-based application designed to automate and streamline the billing processes for net-metered electricity accounts. Built on Spring Boot 3.2.2 framework with Java 21, the system manages the complete lifecycle of billing operations including account registration, meter management, meter reading collection, bill generation, invoice issuance, and fiscal integration with regulatory bodies.
The NMBS supports multiple customer categories including domestic, commercial, and MD commercial accounts, enabling flexible tariff configurations and usage-based billing calculations. The system incorporates robust security mechanisms with role-based access control, comprehensive reporting capabilities, real-time financial management dashboards, and fiscal compliance features. Through its intuitive web interface and RESTful APIs, the syste
output: {"executive_summary": "NET-METERING BILLING SYSTEM NAME OF APPLICATION Net-Metering Billing System (NMBS) 1.0 ABSTRACT The Net-Metering Billing System is a comprehensive, enterprise-grade web-based application designed to automate and streamline the billing processes for net-metered electricity accounts. Built on Spring Boot 3.2.2 framework with Java 21, the system manages the complete lifecycle of billing operations including account registration, meter management, meter reading collection, bill generation, invoice issuance, and fiscal integration with regulatory bodies. The NMBS supports multiple customer categories including domestic, commercial, and MD commercial accounts, enabling flexible tariff configurations and usage-based billing calculations. The system incorporates robust security mechanisms with role-based access control, comprehensive reporting capabilities, real-time financial management dashboards, and fiscal compliance features. Through its intuitive web interface and RESTful APIs, the system serves as a centralized platform for utility providers to efficiently manage net-metering accounts while maintaining data integrity and regulatory compliance. 2.0 INTRODUCTION 2.1 Background Net metering is a billing mechanism that encourages consumers to invest in renewable energy by allowing them to export excess electricity generated back to the grid in exchange for credits. This requires sophisticated billing systems capable of tracking both import and export energy flows, calculating complex tariffs, and generating accurate invoices that reflect the net consumption or net export balance. 2.2 Purpose and Scope The Net-Metering Billing System addresses the critical need for automated billing management in utility companies that operate net-metering programs. The system is designed to: Automate Account Management: Streamline the registration and maintenance of net-metering accounts Track Energy Flows: Monitor both energy imports and exports with precise measurement capabilities Calculate Dynamic Bills: Support complex billing scenarios based on predefined tariff structures Ensure Compliance: Integrate with fiscal authorities to ensure regulatory and tax compliance Provide Visibility: Offer real-time dashboards and detailed reporting for stakeholders 2.3 Technology Stack The system is built using modern, scalable technologies: Backend Framework: Spring Boot 3.2.2 Programming Language: Java 21 Database: MySQL Frontend: Thymeleaf templates with Bootstrap CSS and JavaScript Communication: REST APIs and WebSocket for real-time updates Authentication: Spring Security with role-based access control Deployment: Docker-compatible, cloud-ready architecture 3.0 PROBLEM DEFINITION 3.1 Current Challenges in Net-Metering Billing Challenge 1: Complex Billing Calculations Traditional billing systems are not equipped to handle the bidirectional energy flows inherent in net-metering. They struggle with: Tracking simultaneous imports and exports Calculating time-of-use rates Handling deemed benefits and export credits Managing power factor penalties for commercial accounts Challenge 2: Multi-Category Account Management Billing requirements vary significantly across different customer segments: Domestic accounts require simplified billing with basic net calculation Commercial accounts need detailed invoice breakdowns with VAT calculations MD Commercial accounts require sophisticated time-of-use billing with demand charges Each category demands different tariff structures and calculation engines Challenge 3: Regulatory Compliance Utility companies must: Integrate with fiscal authorities for tax compliance and invoice fiscalization Maintain audit trails for all financial transactions Generate compliant invoices meeting regulatory standards Ensure data integrity and prevent billing fraud Challenge 4: Operational Inefficiency Manual processes lead to: Delayed billing cycles due to manual meter reading collection High error rates in manual calculations Inefficient account onboarding and management Difficulty in generating ad-hoc reports for decision makers 3.2 Solution Requirements The system must provide: Automated, accurate billing calculations supporting multiple account categories Flexible tariff configuration for various customer segments Integration with regulatory bodies for fiscal compliance Real-time visibility into financial metrics and account status Scalable architecture supporting thousands of concurrent accounts Role-based access control with granular permission management Comprehensive audit trails and transaction records User-friendly interfaces for both administrators and customers 4.0 OVERVIEW OF THE DEVELOPED APPLICATION 4.1 System Architecture The Net-Metering Billing System follows a three-tier architecture: Presentation Layer: Thymeleaf-based HTML templates rendering dynamic user interfaces Bootstrap CSS framework for responsive design Real-time updates via WebSocket communication Secure authentication and session management Business Logic Layer: Service layer implementing core billing algorithms Controllers managing request routing and response handling Spring Security implementing authentication and authorization Transaction management ensuring data consistency Persistence Layer: JPA repositories for CRUD operations MySQL database with optimized schema Lazy loading for performance optimization Support for complex queries and reporting 4.2 Core Modules Account Management Module Create and register new billing accounts Update account details and contact information Manage account categories (Domestic, Commercial, MD Commercial) Track account lifecycle from registration to termination Support bulk account import via CSV Meter Management Module Register meters to billing accounts Track meter specifications (manufacturer, model, serial number) Monitor meter status and lifecycle Support multiple meters per account Manage meter capacities and tariff assignments Meter Reading Module Collect meter readings (impor", "sentiment": "Urgent", "entities": ["Metering Billing System", "Support", "Commercial", "Challenge", "Spring Boot"], "topics": [], "action_items": [], "decisions": []}

Built on Spring Boot 3.2.2 framework with Java 21, the system manages the complete lifecycle of billing operations including account registration, meter management, meter reading collection, bill generation, invoice issuance, and fiscal integration with regulatory bodies. The NMBS supports multiple customer categories including domestic, commercial, and MD commercial accounts, enabling flexible tariff configurations and usage-based billing calculations. The system incorporates robust security mechanisms with role-based access control, comprehensive reporting capabilities, real-time financial management dashboards, and fiscal compliance features. Through its intuitive web interface and RESTful APIs, the system serves as a centralized platform for utility providers to efficiently manage net-metering accounts while maintaining data integrity and regulatory compliance. 2.0 INTRODUCTION 2.1 Background Net metering is a billing mechanism that encourages consumers to invest in renewable energy by allowing them to export excess electricity generated back to the grid in exchange for credits. This requires sophisticated billing systems capable of tracking both import and export energy flows, calculating complex tariffs, and generating accurate invoices that reflect the net consumption or net export balance. 2.2 Purpose and Scope The Net-Metering Billing System addresses the critical need for automated billing management in utility companies that operate net-metering programs. The system is designed to: Automate Account Management: Streamline the registration and maintenance of net-metering accounts Track Energy Flows: Monitor both energy imports and exports with precise measurement capabilities Calculate Dynamic Bills: Support complex billing scenarios based on predefined tariff structures Ensure Compliance: Integrate with fiscal authorities to ensure regulatory and tax compliance Provide Visibility: Offer real-time dashboards and detailed reporting for stakeholders 2.3 Technology Stack The system is built using modern, scalable technologies: Backend Framework: Spring Boot 3.2.2 Programming Language: Java 21 Database: MySQL Frontend: Thymeleaf templates with Bootstrap CSS and JavaScript Communication: REST APIs and WebSocket for real-time updates Authentication: Spring Security with role-based access control Deployment: Docker-compatible, cloud-ready architecture 3.0 PROBLEM DEFINITION 3.1 Current Challenges in Net-Metering Billing Challenge 1: Complex Billing Calculations Traditional billing systems are not equipped to handle the bidirectional energy flows inherent in net-metering. They struggle with: Tracking simultaneous imports and exports Calculating time-of-use rates Handling deemed benefits and export credits Managing power factor penalties for commercial accounts Challenge 2: Multi-Category Account Management Billing requirements vary significantly across different customer segments: Domestic accounts require simplified billing with basic net calculation Commercial accounts need detailed invoice breakdowns with VAT calculations MD Commercial accounts require sophisticated time-of-use billing with demand charges Each category demands different tariff structures and calculation engines Challenge 3: Regulatory Compliance Utility companies must: Integrate with fiscal authorities for tax compliance and invoice fiscalization Maintain audit trails for all financial transactions Generate compliant invoices meeting regulatory standards Ensure data integrity and prevent billing fraud Challenge 4: Operational Inefficiency Manual processes lead to: makers 3.2 Solution Requirements The system must provide: Automated, accurate billing calculations supporting multiple account categories Flexible tariff configuration for various customer segments Integration with regulatory bodies for fiscal compliance Real-time visibility into financial metrics and account status Scalable architecture supporting thousands of concurrent accounts Role-based access control with granular permission management Comprehensive audit trails and transaction records User-friendly interfaces for both administrators and customers 4.0 OVERVIEW OF THE DEVELOPED The NMBS supports multiple customer categories including domestic, commercial, and MD commercial accounts, enabling flexible tariff configurations and usage-based billing calculations. The system incorporates robust security mechanisms with role-based access control, comprehensive reporting capabilities, real-time financial management dashboards, and fiscal compliance features. Through its intuitive web interface and RESTful APIs, the system serves as a centralized platform for utility providers to efficiently manage net-metering accounts while maintaining data integrity and regulatory compliance. 2.0 INTRODUCTION 2.1 Background Net metering is a billing mechanism that encourages consumers to invest in renewable energy by allowing them to export excess electricity generated back to the grid in exchange for credits. This requires sophisticated billing systems capable of tracking both import and export energy flows, calculating complex tariffs, and generating accurate invoices that reflect the net consumption or net export balance. 2.2 Purpose and Scope The Net-Metering Billing System addresses the critical need for automated billing management in utility companies that operate net-metering programs. Functional Requirements Specification (FRS) Clarification Guide
Version 1.0



 
Supply and Implementation of ZETDC Billing System
May, 2025
Longshine Technology Group Co., Ltd.


Revision History

Contents

Overview
The purpose of the Billing System is to provide ZETDC and their business users with a set of software infrastructure, standards, guidelines, models, services and processes in delivering an environment that is robust and reliable to satisfy current and future service requirements in an agile manner through exposing re-usable services. The Billing System provides ZETDC with a full set of modules such as customer relationship management, NDPM, contracting management, asset management, metering management, billing management, prepaid billing management, collection management, outage management, dashboard, reports, enterprise application platform, mobile App, self-service Portal and CSM Portal. instruction: Analyze the following document excerpt from '6_ZETDC Metering Manual V1.0_Updated.pdf' (chunk 1 of 24). Extract key entities, topics, sentiment, action items, and decisions.
output: {"executive_summary": "This document excerpt is a user manual for the Metering Module of the ZETDC Utility Management System (Zums). It provides step-by-step instructions on how to perform various metering-related tasks within the system. Key functions covered include accessing and logging into the Zums site, creating and managing routes, creating itineraries, setting up meter reading calendars, linking itineraries to substations or feeders, and allocating premises to itineraries. The manual details the process for creating new routes, searching for existing ones, and associating them with specific Meter Reading Centres (MRCs). It also outlines the steps for creating itineraries, defining their attributes, and linking them to routes and grid resources like transformers or feeders. The document is designed to guide users through the operational aspects of the Zums metering system.", "sentiment": "Neutral", "entities": ["Zums", "ZETDC", "Metering Module", "MRC", "NMD"], "topics": ["Utility Management System", "Metering Module", "User Manual", "Route Management", "Itinerary Management"], "action_items": ["Access the Zums site using the provided URL.", "Log in to the Zums system with your credentials.", "Create a new route by navigating to Metering > Itinerary Management > Route Maintenance."], "decisions": []}

In the event that all readings were not captured, split the itinerary so as to submit read points only and 
focus on the unread at a later stage. 
Capturing Daily Demand Readings 
Metering>>MR Mgmt>>MR Mgmt>>Daily MD MR Query 
 
 
Insert account number , tick organization and query 
 
Select query result and click ”input demand” 
 
 
 
 
Page 24 of 28 
 Select the month and confirm 
 
Capture demand readings 
 
Click save 
 
Follow steps on MR Plan account(account) till readings capture 
 
 
 
Splitting the itinerary 
Expand meter reading data information by increasing from default 5 records to the maximum allowable 
number (currently at 200) 
 
 
 
Page 25 of 28 
 Select the unread points under MR Data info  
 
Click split button  
Confirm splitting  
 
System will confirm split , by creating a new application number . 
Use itinerary number to search for the split batches 
 
Click on query 
 
System will show the two batch numbers, one with readings and the other without.
output: {"executive_summary": "This document excerpt is a user manual for the Metering Module of the ZETDC Utility Management System (Zums). It provides step-by-step instructions on how to perform various metering-related tasks within the system. Key functions covered include accessing and logging into the Zums site, creating and managing routes, creating itineraries, setting up meter reading calendars, linking itineraries to substations or feeders, and allocating premises to itineraries. The manual details the process for creating new routes, searching for existing ones, and associating them with specific Meter Reading Centres (MRCs). It also outlines the steps for creating itineraries, defining their attributes, and linking them to routes and grid resources like transformers or feeders. The document is designed to guide users through the operational aspects of the Zums metering system.", "sentiment": "Neutral", "entities": ["Zums", "ZETDC", "Metering Module", "MRC", "NMD"], "topics": ["Utility Management System", "Metering Module", "User Manual", "Route Management", "Itinerary Management"], "action_items": ["Access the Zums site using the provided URL.", "Log in to the Zums system with your credentials.", "Create a new route by navigating to Metering > Itinerary Management > Route Maintenance."], "decisions": []}

umber to search for the split batches

NET-METERING BILLING SYSTEM
NAME OF APPLICATION
Net-Metering Billing System (NMBS)
1.0 ABSTRACT
The Net-Metering Billing System is a comprehensive, enterprise-grade web-based application designed to automate and streamline the billing processes for net-metered electricity accounts. Built on Spring Boot 3.2.2 framework with Java 21, the system manages the complete lifecycle of billing operations including account registration, meter management, meter reading collection, bill generation, invoice issuance, and fiscal integration with regulatory bodies.
The NMBS supports multiple customer categories including domestic, commercial, and MD commercial accounts, enabling flexible tariff configurations and usage-based billing calculations. The system incorporates robust security mechanisms with role-based access control, comprehensive reporting capabilities, real-time financial management dashboards, and fiscal compliance features. Through its intuitive web interface and RESTful APIs, the syste
output: {"executive_summary": "NET-METERING BILLING SYSTEM NAME OF APPLICATION Net-Metering Billing System (NMBS) 1.0 ABSTRACT The Net-Metering Billing System is a comprehensive, enterprise-grade web-based application designed to automate and streamline the billing processes for net-metered electricity accounts. Built on Spring Boot 3.2.2 framework with Java 21, the system manages the complete lifecycle of billing operations including account registration, meter management, meter reading collection, bill generation, invoice issuance, and fiscal integration with regulatory bodies. The NMBS supports multiple customer categories including domestic, commercial, and MD commercial accounts, enabling flexible tariff configurations and usage-based billing calculations. The system incorporates robust security mechanisms with role-based access control, comprehensive reporting capabilities, real-time financial management dashboards, and fiscal compliance features. Through its intuitive web interface and RESTful APIs, the system serves as a centralized platform for utility providers to efficiently manage net-metering accounts while maintaining data integrity and regulatory compliance. 2.0 INTRODUCTION 2.1 Background Net metering is a billing mechanism that encourages consumers to invest in renewable energy by allowing them to export excess electricity generated back to the grid in exchange for credits. This requires sophisticated billing systems capable of tracking both import and export energy flows, calculating complex tariffs, and generating accurate invoices that reflect the net consumption or net export balance. 2.2 Purpose and Scope The Net-Metering Billing System addresses the critical need for automated billing management in utility companies that operate net-metering programs. The system is designed to: Automate Account Management: Streamline the registration and maintenance of net-metering accounts Track Energy Flows: Monitor both energy imports and exports with precise measurement capabilities Calculate Dynamic Bills: Support complex billing scenarios based on predefined tariff structures Ensure Compliance: Integrate with fiscal authorities to ensure regulatory and tax compliance Provide Visibility: Offer real-time dashboards and detailed reporting for stakeholders 2.3 Technology Stack The system is built using modern, scalable technologies: Backend Framework: Spring Boot 3.2.2 Programming Language: Java 21 Database: MySQL Frontend: Thymeleaf templates with Bootstrap CSS and JavaScript Communication: REST APIs and WebSocket for real-time updates Authentication: Spring Security with role-based access control Deployment: Docker-compatible, cloud-ready architecture 3.0 PROBLEM DEFINITION 3.1 Current Challenges in Net-Metering Billing Challenge 1: Complex Billing Calculations Traditional billing systems are not equipped to handle the bidirectional energy flows inherent in net-metering. They struggle with: Tracking simultaneous imports and exports Calculating time-of-use rates Handling deemed benefits and export credits Managing power factor penalties for commercial accounts Challenge 2: Multi-Category Account Management Billing requirements vary significantly across different customer segments: Domestic accounts require simplified billing with basic net calculation Commercial accounts need detailed invoice breakdowns with VAT calculations MD Commercial accounts require sophisticated time-of-use billing with demand charges Each category demands different tariff structures and calculation engines Challenge 3: Regulatory Compliance Utility companies must: Integrate with fiscal authorities for tax compliance and invoice fiscalization Maintain audit trails for all financial transactions Generate compliant invoices meeting regulatory standards Ensure data integrity and prevent billing fraud Challenge 4: Operational Inefficiency Manual processes lead to: Delayed billing cycles due to manual meter reading collection High error rates in manual calculations Inefficient account onboarding and management Difficulty in generating ad-hoc reports for decision makers 3.2 Solution Requirements The system must provide: Automated, accurate billing calculations supporting multiple account categories Flexible tariff configuration for various customer segments Integration with regulatory bodies for fiscal compliance Real-time visibility into financial metrics and account status Scalable architecture supporting thousands of concurrent accounts Role-based access control with granular permission management Comprehensive audit trails and transaction records User-friendly interfaces for both administrators and customers 4.0 OVERVIEW OF THE DEVELOPED APPLICATION 4.1 System Architecture The Net-Metering Billing System follows a three-tier architecture: Presentation Layer: Thymeleaf-based HTML templates rendering dynamic user interfaces Bootstrap CSS framework for responsive design Real-time updates via WebSocket communication Secure authentication and session management Business Logic Layer: Service layer implementing core billing algorithms Controllers managing request routing and response handling Spring Security implementing authentication and authorization Transaction management ensuring data consistency Persistence Layer: JPA repositories for CRUD operations MySQL database with optimized schema Lazy loading for performance optimization Support for complex queries and reporting 4.2 Core Modules Account Management Module Create and register new billing accounts Update account details and contact information Manage account categories (Domestic, Commercial, MD Commercial) Track account lifecycle from registration to termination Support bulk account import via CSV Meter Management Module Register meters to billing accounts Track meter specifications (manufacturer, model, serial number) Monitor meter status and lifecycle Support multiple meters per account Manage meter capacities and tariff assignments Meter Reading Module Collect meter readings (impor", "sentiment": "Urgent", "entities": ["Metering Billing System", "Support", "Commercial", "Challenge", "Spring Boot"], "topics": [], "action_items": [], "decisions": []}

Built on Spring Boot 3.2.2 framework with Java 21, the system manages the complete lifecycle of billing operations including account registration, meter management, meter reading collection, bill generation, invoice issuance, and fiscal integration with regulatory bodies. The NMBS supports multiple customer categories including domestic, commercial, and MD commercial accounts, enabling flexible tariff configurations and usage-based billing calculations. The system incorporates robust security mechanisms with role-based access control, comprehensive reporting capabilities, real-time financial management dashboards, and fiscal compliance features. Through its intuitive web interface and RESTful APIs, the system serves as a centralized platform for utility providers to efficiently manage net-metering accounts while maintaining data integrity and regulatory compliance. 2.0 INTRODUCTION 2.1 Background Net metering is a billing mechanism that encourages consumers to invest in renewable energy by allowing them to export excess electricity generated back to the grid in exchange for credits. This requires sophisticated billing systems capable of tracking both import and export energy flows, calculating complex tariffs, and generating accurate invoices that reflect the net consumption or net export balance. 2.2 Purpose and Scope The Net-Metering Billing System addresses the critical need for automated billing management in utility companies that operate net-metering programs. The system is designed to: Automate Account Management: Streamline the registration and maintenance of net-metering accounts Track Energy Flows: Monitor both energy imports and exports with precise measurement capabilities Calculate Dynamic Bills: Support complex billing scenarios based on predefined tariff structures Ensure Compliance: Integrate with fiscal authorities to ensure regulatory and tax compliance Provide Visibility: Offer real-time dashboards and detailed reporting for stakeholders 2.3 Technology Stack The system is built using modern, scalable technologies: Backend Framework: Spring Boot 3.2.2 Programming Language: Java 21 Database: MySQL Frontend: Thymeleaf templates with Bootstrap CSS and JavaScript Communication: REST APIs and WebSocket for real-time updates Authentication: Spring Security with role-based access control Deployment: Docker-compatible, cloud-ready architecture 3.0 PROBLEM DEFINITION 3.1 Current Challenges in Net-Metering Billing Challenge 1: Complex Billing Calculations Traditional billing systems are not equipped to handle the bidirectional energy flows inherent in net-metering. They struggle with: Tracking simultaneous imports and exports Calculating time-of-use rates Handling deemed benefits and export credits Managing power factor penalties for commercial accounts Challenge 2: Multi-Category Account Management Billing requirements vary significantly across different customer segments: Domestic accounts require simplified billing with basic net calculation Commercial accounts need detailed invoice breakdowns with VAT calculations MD Commercial accounts require sophisticated time-of-use billing with demand charges Each category demands different tariff structures and calculation engines Challenge 3: Regulatory Compliance Utility companies must: Integrate with fiscal authorities for tax compliance and invoice fiscalization Maintain audit trails for all financial transactions Generate compliant invoices meeting regulatory standards Ensure data integrity and prevent billing fraud Challenge 4: Operational Inefficiency Manual processes lead to: Delayed billing cycles due to manual meter reading collection High error rates in manual calculations Inefficient account onboarding and management Difficulty in generating ad-hoc reports for decision makers 3.2 Solution Requirements The system must provide: Automated, accurate billing calculations supporting multiple account categories Flexible tariff configuration for various customer segments Integration with regulatory bodies for fiscal compliance Real-time visibility into financial metrics and account status Scalable architecture supporting thousands of concurrent accounts Role-based access control with granular permission management Comprehensive audit trails and transaction records User-friendly interfaces for both administrators and customers 4.0 OVERVIEW OF THE DEVELOPED This requires sophisticated billing systems capable of tracking both import and export energy flows, calculating complex tariffs, and generating accurate invoices that reflect the net consumption or net export balance. 2.2 Purpose and Scope The Net-Metering Billing System addresses the critical need for automated billing management in utility companies that operate net-metering programs. output: {"executive_summary": "NET-METERING BILLING SYSTEM NAME OF APPLICATION Net-Metering Billing System (NMBS) 1.0 ABSTRACT The Net-Metering Billing System is a comprehensive, enterprise-grade web-based application designed to automate and streamline the billing processes for net-metered electricity accounts. Built on Spring Boot 3.2.2 framework with Java 21, the system manages the complete lifecycle of billing operations including account registration, meter management, meter reading collection, bill generation, invoice issuance, and fiscal integration with regulatory bodies. The NMBS supports multiple customer categories including domestic, commercial, and MD commercial accounts, enabling flexible tariff configurations and usage-based billing calculations. The system incorporates robust security mechanisms with role-based access control, comprehensive reporting capabilities, real-time financial management dashboards, and fiscal compliance features. Through its intuitive web interface and RESTful APIs, the system serves as a centralized platform for utility providers to efficiently manage net-metering accounts while maintaining data integrity and regulatory compliance. 2.0 INTRODUCTION 2.1 Background Net metering is a billing mechanism that encourages consumers to invest in renewable energy by allowing them to export excess electricity generated back to the grid in exchange for credits. This requires sophisticated billing systems capable of tracking both import and export energy flows, calculating complex tariffs, and generating accurate invoices that reflect the net consumption or net export balance. 2.2 Purpose and Scope The Net-Metering Billing System addresses the critical need for automated billing management in utility companies that operate net-metering programs. The system is designed to: Automate Account Management: Streamline the registration and maintenance of net-metering accounts Track Energy Flows: Monitor both energy imports and exports with precise measurement capabilities Calculate Dynamic Bills: Support complex billing scenarios based on predefined tariff structures Ensure Compliance: Integrate with fiscal authorities to ensure regulatory and tax compliance Provide Visibility: Offer real-time dashboards and detailed reporting for stakeholders 2.3 Technology Stack The system is built using modern, scalable technologies: Backend Framework: Spring Boot 3.2.2 Programming Language: Java 21 Database: MySQL Frontend: Thymeleaf templates with Bootstrap CSS and JavaScript Communication: REST APIs and WebSocket for real-time updates Authentication: Spring Security with role-based access control Deployment: Docker-compatible, cloud-ready architecture 3.0 PROBLEM DEFINITION 3.1 Current Challenges in Net-Metering Billing Challenge 1: Complex Billing Calculations Traditional billing systems are not equipped to handle the bidirectional energy flows inherent in net-metering. They struggle with: Tracking simultaneous imports and exports Calculating time-of-use rates Handling deemed benefits and export credits Managing power factor penalties for commercial accounts Commercial accounts need detailed invoice breakdowns with VAT calculations MD Commercial accounts require sophisticated time-of-use billing with demand charges Each category demands different tariff structures and calculation engines Challenge 3: Regulatory Compliance Utility companies must: Integrate with fiscal authorities for tax compliance and invoice fiscalization Maintain audit trails for all financial transactions Generate compliant invoices meeting regulatory standards Ensure data integrity and prevent billing fraud Challenge 4: Operational Inefficiency Manual processes lead to: Delayed billing cycles due to manual meter reading collection High error rates in manual calculations Inefficient account onboarding and management Difficulty in generating ad-hoc reports for decision makers 3.2 Solution Requirements The system must provide: Automated, accurate billing calculations supporting multiple account categories Flexible tariff configuration for various customer segments Integration with regulatory bodies for fiscal compliance Real-time visibility into financial metrics and account status Scalable architecture supporting thousands of concurrent accounts Role-based access control with granular permission management Comprehensive audit trails and transaction records User-friendly interfaces for both administrators and customers 4.0 OVERVIEW OF THE DEVELOPED This requires sophisticated billing systems capable of tracking both import and export energy flows, calculating complex tariffs, and generating accurate invoices that reflect the net consumption or net export balance. 2.2 Purpose and Scope The Net-Metering Billing System addresses the critical need for automated billing management in utility companies that operate net-metering programs. The system is designed to: Automate Account Management: Streamline the registration and maintenance of net-metering accounts Track Energy Flows: Monitor both energy imports and exports with precise measurement capabilities Calculate Dynamic Bills: Support complex billing scenarios based on predefined tariff structures Ensure Compliance: Integrate with fiscal authorities to ensure regulatory and tax compliance Provide Visibility: Offer real-time dashboards and detailed reporting for stakeholders 2.3 Technology Stack The system is built using modern, scalable technologies: Backend Framework: Spring Boot 3.2.2 Programming Language: Java 21 Database: MySQL Frontend: Thymeleaf templates with Bootstrap CSS and JavaScript Communication: REST APIs and WebSocket for real-time updates Authentication: Spring Security with role-based access control Deployment: Docker-compatible, cloud-ready architecture 3.0 PROBLEM DEFINITION 3.1 Current Challenges in Net-Metering Billing Challenge 1: Complex Billing Calculations Traditional billing systems are not equipped to handle the bidirectional energy flows inherent in net-metering. makers 3.2 Solution Requirements The system must provide: Automated, accurate billing calculations supporting multiple account categories Flexible tariff configuration for various customer segments Integration with regulatory bodies for fiscal compliance Real-time visibility into financial metrics and account status Scalable architecture supporting thousands of concurrent accounts Role-based This requires sophisticated billing systems capable of tracking both import and export energy flows, calculating complex tariffs, and generating accurate invoices that reflect the net consumption or net export balance. 2.2 Purpose and Scope The Net-Metering Billing System addresses the critical need for automated billing management in utility companies that operate net-metering programs. output: {"executive_summary": "NET-METERING BILLING SYSTEM NAME OF APPLICATION Net-Metering Billing System (NMBS) 1.0 ABSTRACT The Net-Metering Billing System is a comprehensive, enterprise-grade web-based application designed to automate and streamline the billing processes for net-metered electricity accounts. Built on Spring Boot 3.2.2 framework with Java 21, the system manages the complete lifecycle of billing operations including account registration, meter management, meter reading collection, bill generation, invoice issuance, and fiscal integration with regulatory bodies. The NMBS supports multiple customer categories including domestic, commercial, and MD commercial accounts, enabling flexible tariff configurations and usage-based billing calculations. The system incorporates robust security mechanisms with role-based access control, comprehensive reporting capabilities, real-time financial management dashboards, and fiscal compliance features. Through its intuitive web interface and RESTful APIs, the system serves as a centralized platform for utility providers to efficiently manage net-metering accounts while maintaining data integrity and regulatory compliance. 2.0 INTRODUCTION 2.1 Background Net metering is a billing mechanism that encourages consumers to invest in renewable energy by allowing them to export excess electricity generated back to the grid in exchange for credits. This requires sophisticated billing systems capable of tracking both import and export energy flows, calculating complex tariffs, and generating accurate invoices that reflect the net consumption or net export balance. 2.2 Purpose and Scope The Net-Metering Billing System addresses the critical need for automated billing management in utility companies that operate net-metering programs. The system is designed to: Automate Account Management: Streamline the registration and maintenance of net-metering accounts Track Energy Flows: Monitor both energy imports and exports with precise measurement capabilities Calculate Dynamic Bills: Support complex billing scenarios based on predefined tariff structures Ensure Compliance: Integrate with fiscal authorities to ensure regulatory and tax compliance Provide Visibility: Offer real-time dashboards and detailed reporting for stakeholders 2.3 Technology Stack The system is built using modern, scalable technologies: Backend Framework: Spring Boot 3.2.2 Programming Language: Java 21 Database: MySQL Frontend: Thymeleaf templates with Bootstrap CSS and JavaScript Communication: REST APIs and WebSocket for real-time updates Authentication: Spring Security with role-based access control Deployment: Docker-compatible, cloud-ready architecture 3.0 PROBLEM DEFINITION 3.1 Current Challenges in Net-Metering Billing Challenge 1: Complex Billing Calculations Traditional billing systems are not equipped to handle the bidirectional energy flows inherent in net-metering. They struggle with: Tracking simultaneous imports and exports Calculating time-of-use rates Handling deemed benefits and export credits Managing power factor penalties for commercial accounts Challenge 2: Multi-Category Account Management Billing requirements vary significantly across different customer segments: Domestic accounts require simplified billing with basic net calculation Commercial accounts need detailed invoice breakdowns with VAT calculations MD Commercial accounts require sophisticated time-of-use billing with demand charges Each category demands different tariff structures and calculation engines Challenge 3: Regulatory Compliance Utility companies must: Integrate with fiscal authorities for tax compliance and invoice fiscalization Maintain audit trails for all financial transactions Generate compliant invoices meeting regulatory standards Ensure data integrity and prevent billing fraud Challenge 4: Operational Inefficiency Manual processes lead to: Delayed billing cycles due to manual meter reading collection High error rates in manual calculations Inefficient account onboarding and management Difficulty in generating ad-hoc reports for decision makers 3.2 Solution Requirements The system must provide: Automated, accurate billing calculations supporting multiple account categories Flexible tariff configuration for various customer segments Integration with regulatory bodies for fiscal compliance Real-time visibility into financial metrics and account status Scalable architecture supporting thousands of concurrent accounts Role-based access control with granular permission management Comprehensive audit trails and transaction records User-friendly interfaces for both administrators and customers 4.0 OVERVIEW OF THE DEVELOPED
